description: Lock Putting-Out Textile System to domestic textile out-work coordinated by clothiers/merchants, not dye-guild regulation, price bulletins, or courier networks.

checks.reason: The source-backed system is domestic textile production by dependent out-workers.
Domestic household textile work is core to the putting-out production method.
The source-backed clothier/out-worker relationship includes payment obligations and debt litigation.
Contract law enables obligations, payments, debts, and remedies without being the textile production method.
Commercial dispute enforcement is the contextual legal layer for textile-related debts.
Merchant courts are related commercial enforcement context, not a strict production prerequisite.
Dye guilds should not be restored unless the node is narrowed to dyeing and finishing regulation.
Market price bulletins are not the documented mechanism in the source-backed putting-out scope.
Courier networks should not carry the direct source-backed putting-out relationship.
The node remains anchored to late-medieval English and European textile putting-out evidence.
